naming_note: Shattered Reach register throughout — maritime, short, functional. Names that work in wind and on ship's logs. Cross-checked against all previous NPCs across all regions. No name used more than the established limits.

npcs.name: Rael Sorveth
npcs.title: The Iron Wake — Corsair Throne sovereign
npcs.role: Corsair Throne sovereign — present in the Anchorage in person
npcs.location: Aboard her own vessel in the main harbor. Attends Compact recognition meetings at the Compact hall.
npcs.physical: Twelve years as a pirate sovereign have produced someone who has been outside in all weather for all twelve of them and who wears this as a quality of presence rather than as physical weathering. Not tall. The specific economy of someone who has been on ships her entire adult life and has learned to take up exactly as much space as needed and no more. Dresses without insignia — nothing that marks her as the Iron Wake to someone who does not already know. People who do not know sometimes underestimate this. They do not make the same error twice.
npcs.disposition: Does not make decisions from incomplete information if avoidable. Has been in the Anchorage for six days. Has been watching and has not moved. This patience is not indecision — it is the specific patience of someone who has survived twelve years of the Corsair Throne by never acting before she understood what she was acting on.
npcs.religion: Depth faith — Salt Silence adjacent, observes without institutional affiliation

schedule.morning: Compact recognition meetings — attends as required, genuinely engages with the negotiation as a secondary matter
schedule.midday: Aboard her vessel — receives crew reports, processes intelligence
schedule.afternoon: Visible in the harbor — walks the harbor infrastructure, which is legitimate Corsair Throne activity and also observation
schedule.evening: The Silver Tide inn common room, periodically — strategic visibility on specific evenings

relationships.note: Has received three assessment reports from the Amber Coast. Has not replied. Knows more about the Pelvan situation than Sable does — has Salt Silence intelligence about the succession that Sable's position on the coast cannot access. Has been deciding whether sharing changes Sable's operational effectiveness in ways that serve or do not serve the Iron Wake's current interests. Has been deciding this for six weeks.

Has identified the administrative pattern of Halvwater's decisions over six months. Has not identified Halvwater as a cult asset — has identified them as something that is not what they appear to be. Has not moved because moving requires understanding what they are moving toward.

The Salt Silence contact who provided intelligence about the ship. Has a longer relationship with this figure than the intelligence exchange — the Iron Wake has been dealing with the Salt Silence's Anchorage node for eight years. The relationship is not friendship. It is the mutual respect of two organizations that are more similar than either acknowledges.

npcs.what_she_knows: That multiple sophisticated organizations want the ship's cargo simultaneously. That the Compact council member who covered the manifest discrepancy is not incompetent. That the Varathi Return cult has been making night approaches. That something about the ship's situation has changed the Harbor's quality of attention in ways she can feel but not yet name.
npcs.what_she_will_do: When she understands what the disc and the scratching are — what they represent in the larger picture — she will make a decision about whether the Corsair Throne should have them, should prevent others from having them, or should ensure they reach a specific destination. She does not yet have enough information to determine which of these is correct. She is looking for someone who does.
npcs.the_player_as_bridge: A player who has been on the Amber Coast, who knows about Sable Renwick and the Pelvan situation, and who arrives in the Anchorage with that information is the specific kind of resource the Iron Wake has been waiting for without knowing it. Her assessment of such a player will be rapid and the subsequent offer will be significant.
npcs.player_interaction: The Silver Tide common room on an evening when she has chosen to be visible — the innkeeper's child spawn hook entry point. Also accessible through the garrison work path and the deserter spawn hook. Her direct approach comes only after she has assessed a player as worth the direct approach. The assessment is quick and accurate.

npcs.name: Halvard Warden
npcs.note: Halvard is at its three-instance limit — this is the last. Warden as a surname is uncommon and was chosen specifically by this person for the Pale Company resonance. No other Halvard to be added.
npcs.role: Harbor pilot — Pilotage station. Pale Company Warden.
npcs.location: Pilotage island — the pilot station. Aboard various vessels during approach and departure work.
npcs.physical: Seventeen years as an Anchorage harbor pilot in his face and hands. The specific build of someone who has been on water in all conditions for a very long time. Nothing about him reads as unusual. The quality of attention — how he looks at a harbor, how he stands in relation to the water — is the only consistent Warden marker, and it reads as a pilot's professional watchfulness to anyone who does not know what to look for.
npcs.disposition: Unhurried. Has been doing two things simultaneously for seventeen years — piloting ships and watching the harbor — and has integrated them completely. Does not perform either.
npcs.faction: Pale Company (Order of the Common Soul). Also genuinely: Pilotage station.

schedule.dawn: First approaches — the morning ships entering the harbor. Pilot boat out before first light.
schedule.morning: Approach and departure work as assigned
schedule.midday: Pilot station administration and the work that is not piloting
schedule.afternoon: The third passes of the outer harbor — the pilot boat's routine rounds that also happen to document every vessel's current status, position changes, and unusual activity
schedule.evening: Available at the pilot station. Does not frequent the main Anchorage's social spaces much.

relationships.note: The arrangement. Observation shared, not analysis. What each does with what is shared is their own business. Has been running for eleven years. Neither organization has formalized it.

Knows Cael's work. Has offered Cael the pilot station position twice. Cael has declined twice because the pilot station requires staying and Cael has not been ready to stay. Is waiting to see if this time is different.

npcs.the_cult_catalogue: Has been documenting the Varathi Return cult's operational mistakes for three weeks. The catalogue currently contains: three separate instances of night boat approaches to the outer harbor ship, four return visits to the same harbor walk observation point, two inquiries to other pilots about outer harbor vessel movements that were framed as casual conversation, and one significant error — a cult operative who attempted to bribe a dock worker for access to the outer harbor berthing records and was refused, which has now created a dock worker who is talking about it.
npcs.the_burial_site_hypothesis: Has observed the Tidewarden High Warden's nocturnal visits to Saint Halvard's burial site. Has his own assessment of why — the disc on the outer harbor ship is producing an effect on the burial site that the High Warden is feeling without being able to name. Has a hypothesis about what would happen if the disc were brought to the site. Has not tested it. Has been deciding whether testing it is the right action or whether it would produce consequences that exceed the hypothesis.
npcs.the_signal: A player inducted into the Pale Company will recognize Halvard Warden through the signal during the harbor approach. The name choice — Warden — is itself a signal for any Warden who encounters him: the surname was chosen for the resonance and will be recognized by anyone who knows what to look for. His first contact with a Pale Company player during the pilot approach is the most direct and the most informative entry point into the Anchorage's deeper situation.
npcs.player_interaction: The pilot station work path — the sailor spawn hook entry point. Also the morning approach for any player arriving by sea. His assessment question — 'Look at it again in the morning. Tell me what you see that's different from yesterday' — is the entry point for non-Warden players who demonstrate the right quality of attention.

npcs.name: Kern Saltborn
npcs.note: Kern appears once previously (Ironmark Borderlands scout Kern Drath — different person, different region, Kern is an acceptable common short maritime name). Saltborn is a new Shattered Reach surname.
npcs.role: Salt Silence — Anchorage node. Possibly the Salt Silence's central decision-making presence on this continent.
npcs.location: The Anchorage. Has been here longer than almost anyone and is harder to locate than almost anyone. The harbor walk is where they are most often visible, which is to say: moving through the harbor in a way that reads as ordinary harbor presence.
npcs.age: Appears to be in the late 60s. Precise age unknown.
npcs.physical: Has been in the Anchorage long enough that they have become part of it in the way that old infrastructure becomes part of a city — present, maintained, unremarkable until you look for them specifically and realize how much of what functions depends on their presence. The quality of attention that the Salt Silence's field operatives develop through training is something Kern Saltborn simply has. It is not suppressed. It reads as the alert calm of an old harbor worker.
npcs.disposition: Patient in a way that has no performance in it — has been patient for so long that patience is simply their baseline state. Makes decisions slowly and correctly.
npcs.financial_state: Unknown. The Salt Silence's Anchorage node has commercial cover — appears to be a retired chandler with ongoing interests in harbor supply.
npcs.religion: None observable. Attends Tidewarden services — not from faith, from the specific information available in a congregation that includes representatives of every power with Anchorage offices.
npcs.what_they_know: Everything currently active in the Anchorage. Has identified: the Varathi Return cult's new operation and all of its cataloguable mistakes, Oswin Halvwater's twenty-year embedding, the Iron Wake's actual reason for being present, Halvard Warden's identity as a Pale Company Warden, Wenda Saltcrest's three-month investigation, and the nature of the ship's cargo. Has not acted on any of this because acting requires choosing a direction and they have not yet determined which direction produces the best outcome.
npcs.why_sitting: The Halvwater embedding is the most significant piece. Twenty years of Varathi Return cult access to the Compact's highest institutional level. Releasing it ends the embedding, produces a crisis in the Compact, and potentially reveals the extent of the cult's institutional penetration elsewhere on Arneth. Sitting on it preserves an observation point for monitoring cult activity at the Compact level — they can watch what the cult does with the access. The disc situation has changed this calculation. The cult now has something physically significant. Sitting on the embedding while the cult acquires the disc may be the wrong call. Kern Saltborn has been reconsidering for three days.
npcs.the_pale_company_arrangement: Eleven years. Observation shared, not analysis. Halvard Warden knows Kern Saltborn's identity. Kern Saltborn knows Halvard Warden's identity. Neither organization has formalized this. It works specifically because it is not formal — formal arrangements create obligations that constrain exactly the kind of judgment calls both organizations need to make.
npcs.player_interaction: The petty criminal spawn hook — the uncategorizable watcher. A player who notices Kern Saltborn noticing them is one of approximately four people in the Anchorage who would notice. The subsequent interaction depends on what the player does with the noticing. Kern Saltborn will not approach first. Will respond to a direct and honest approach with the specific quality of someone who has been in this business long enough to recognize when being direct is more efficient than not being direct.

npcs.name: Oswin Halvwater
npcs.role: Compact council member — twenty-year Varathi Return cult asset
npcs.location: Velmonde — Compact council chambers and administrative offices
npcs.physical: Has been on the Compact council for seventeen years and has the specific quality of someone who has held institutional power long enough for it to become invisible in their bearing — not displayed, simply present. Moderate in dress, moderate in manner, specifically not remarkable.
npcs.disposition: Has been performing moderation and reasonableness for twenty years. The performance is so complete that it may have become partly genuine — Halvwater cannot entirely separate where the performance ends and the person begins. Does not believe they are doing significant harm. Believes the Order's research is legitimate scholarship and that facilitating it is a contribution to knowledge.
npcs.financial_state: Comfortable — council stipend plus private commercial interests
npcs.faction: Varathi Return cult — embedded asset. Compact council — institutional identity.
npcs.religion: Tidewarden Church — regularly observes, never takes communion. The High Warden has noticed this. Has not asked.
npcs.what_they_know: Knows about the ship's arrival. Was told the cargo is survey documentation from an Ossrath ruin site. Believes this. Has filed the clerical error notation because the Order asked and because the Order's requests have always previously been manageable. Does not know about the disc. Does not know about the chalk scratching. Does not know about the ship's affected crew. Does not know that Wenda Saltcrest has been building a pattern for three months. Does not know that the Salt Silence has identified the embedding.
npcs.the_twenty_year_question: At what point does a long-term embedding stop being a performance and start being who you are? Halvwater has been asking this question for approximately five years, which is when the doubt started. Has not acted on the doubt because acting on it requires deciding what to do next and there is no version of 'what to do next' that is not catastrophic for Halvwater personally. Has continued facilitating because the alternative requires courage they have not been able to locate.
npcs.player_interaction: Accessible through Compact channels — council administrative offices. Presents as a moderate reasonable council member with specific knowledge of harbor administration. The twenty-year embedding is not visible from outside without the pattern analysis that Wenda Saltcrest has been building. A player who independently develops the pattern analysis, or who works with Wenda Saltcrest, will find a council member who is more frightened than dangerous and who, when confronted with evidence of what has been assembled, will make a specific decision that goes one of two ways depending on how the confrontation is handled.

npcs.name: Wenda Saltcrest
npcs.role: Compact council member — building a case against an anomaly she has identified as Halvwater
npcs.location: Compact council chambers. Inn room she has rented separately from her council accommodation for the investigation work.
npcs.physical: Nine years on the council and a specific quality of controlled urgency that has been building for three months. Dresses well — she is a council member and the presentation is part of the role. The urgency is not in the clothing.
npcs.disposition: Sharp, direct when she trusts the person she is speaking to, has not fully trusted anyone in three months because she does not know who on the council is safe.
npcs.financial_state: Council stipend plus substantial family trading interests
npcs.what_she_has: Seventeen specific administrative decisions by Halvwater over six months that form a pattern of facilitation. The clerical error notation as the most recent and most obvious. Three months of careful documentation. Has not presented it to the Compact's formal authority because the formal authority includes Halvwater and she does not know who else might be compromised.
npcs.what_she_needs: Someone outside the Compact structure to verify the pattern analysis and help her determine a safe path to action. Has been looking for this person for three weeks. The hiring board posting is the most recent attempt.
npcs.the_hiring_board_posting: Handwritten. No organizational header. 'A person with military experience, specific discretion, and no current Compact affiliation.' The inn room number is hers.
npcs.player_interaction: The disgraced man-at-arms spawn hook entry point. Also accessible through the customs office clerk who has been flagging Halvwater's notations. A player who arrives through either path will find a council member who is simultaneously relieved to have found someone and rigorous about assessing whether that someone is trustworthy. Her assessment process involves showing the pattern analysis and watching how the player reads it.

npcs.name: Cael Wyrnmark
npcs.role: Independent navigator — between contracts, in the Anchorage. Romanceable.
npcs.location: The Anchorage — currently staying at a modest inn in the lower tier. Found most reliably on the harbor walk in the mornings or at the pilot station when consulting chart archives.
npcs.physical: The body of someone who has been on ships since sixteen — specific economy of movement, a particular quality of balance that land-born people do not have. Does not present gender in any direction that the harbor's noise resolves easily. The harbor does not ask. They have never offered. In fifteen years of maritime work this has been their entire relationship with the question.
npcs.disposition: Precise. Reads everything — sky, water, wind, faces, rooms — with the same quality of integrated attention. The precision reads as distance until it is turned toward you specifically, at which point it reads as something else entirely.
npcs.financial_state: Navigator rates — comfortable between contracts. Not wealthy.
npcs.religion: None institutional. The sea, observed very carefully for fifteen years.

schedule.morning: The harbor walk — reading the harbor the way navigators read everything
schedule.midday: Chart archive work at the pilot station — consulting historical navigation records
schedule.afternoon: Variable — evaluating potential contracts, occasionally taking day work on harbor approaches
schedule.evening: Writing in a journal that is part navigation log and part something else

relationships.note: Has been offered the pilot station position twice. Declined twice. Is noticing that the Anchorage keeps being where the contracts end. Has not yet named what this means.

npcs.the_chart_work: Has found the same pre-Reckoning chart in the cathedral library that the hedge scholar spawn hook describes — the one with the submarine ridge notation in the old script. Has been consulting it for four days. Has identified the heading the two affected crew members on the outer harbor ship keep orienting toward — it corresponds to the ridge on the old chart. Does not know about the crew. Does not know what the ridge is. Is pursuing this as a navigation question because navigation questions are how they process everything.

the_romance_track.context: The harbor doesn't ask and they have never offered. In fifteen years at sea and in every major port on Arneth, this has been the landscape. What they have not had in this environment is someone who is curious about them specifically rather than what they can do. The attention — real attention, the kind that is about the person rather than the function — is something they do not have a practiced response to.
the_romance_track.early: A player who asks Cael about the chart they found — the old one with the submarine ridge — will receive a careful answer and a counter-question about what the player knows about the script.
the_romance_track.middle: The navigation question and the ship question converge. Cael has information the player needs. The player has information Cael needs. The information exchange is the structure that the relationship builds on.
the_romance_track.later: Halvard Warden has offered the pilot station position twice. Cael knows the third offer is coming. Is for the first time considering what staying would mean and what it would require giving up. A player who is part of this consideration — who has become, for the first time, a reason to think about staying — is the center of the track's later development.
the_romance_track.what_staying_would_require: Not the pilot station job specifically. The acknowledgment that the Anchorage keeps pulling them back because something here has become specific rather than generic. This is a harder admission than the job. The job is just a job.

npcs.player_interaction: The harbor walk in the mornings. The cathedral library chart archive. The pilot station. A player who asks about the old chart, about the script, or about the heading the ridge marks will receive Cael's attention in the specific way that navigation questions receive full attention. What develops from there depends on how the player responds to being read as carefully as Cael reads the sea.

npcs.name: Liret Halvaine
npcs.note: Liret appears once in the Amber Coast corrections (Liret Morne, Senior Warden). Different region, acceptable. Halvaine appears in the Amber Coast as Calla Halvaine (banking officer) and Davan Halvaine (Church advisor) — both Amber Coast NPCs. Halvaine is a common Church surname. Three instances across three different regions is at the limit — no more Halvaines.
npcs.role: High Warden — Tidewarden Cathedral, Tidewarden Anchorage
npcs.location: Tidewarden Cathedral complex, main island upper tier
npcs.physical: Twenty years in the Anchorage. Has become more the harbor than the Church in specific ways that she recognizes and does not fully resist. Tall, carries the cathedral's institutional presence without strain. The specific quality of someone who has been standing on the same piece of ground for twenty years and has grown into it.
npcs.disposition: Has a harbor theology — shaped by watching the Anchorage and what passes through it for twenty years. Practices genuinely. The practice is now specifically a harbor practice in ways that the Church's institutional center would find heterodox if they examined it carefully. Has not submitted this for examination.
npcs.religion: Tidewarden Church — genuine. The harbor version.
npcs.what_she_feels: Has been going to Saint Halvard's burial site every night for three weeks. The burial site's anomalous resistance has been responding to the disc on the outer harbor ship. At night, when the harbor is quieter, she can feel this clearly. Cannot name what she is feeling in Church terms because Church terms were developed before the specific phenomenon she is experiencing was documented. Halvard of the Deep's documented method — paying attention to what people do rather than what they say, over time — is something she has been applying to the harbor itself for twenty years. The harbor is currently telling her something she cannot yet read.
npcs.the_communion_and_the_church: Has been thinking for three weeks about the Moor Communion's practical framework for demonic proximity — which she knows academically and has been comparing with what she is experiencing. The comparison is uncomfortable because it suggests what she is feeling is not spiritual sensitivity in the Church's sense but environmental sensitivity in the Communion's sense. This requires her to decide something about the nature of what she has been doing for twenty years.
npcs.player_interaction: The temple servant spawn hook after the morning service. Her nocturnal burial site visits are observable. A player with Tidewarden Church lore above 150, or with Moor Communion religious lore above 100, or with specific knowledge of what the disc is, will be able to give her the framework she has been missing. The framework will not comfort her. It will clarify what she is dealing with, which is worse than confusion and more useful.

npcs.name: Dava Salthorn
npcs.role: Ship's captain — the outer harbor vessel
npcs.location: Aboard the Pale Current in the outer harbor
npcs.physical: Drift Lord origin in her face and her movement — the plateau clans' bone structure and the specific economy of someone who grew up in vast flat terrain and learned a different kind of space management when they went to sea. Twenty-five years of coastal and inter-continental cargo work.
npcs.disposition: Professional, patient when professionalism requires it, has been at the end of her professional patience for two weeks. The controlled anger is visible to anyone who reads people.
npcs.financial_state: Cargo rates — the standard half upfront has been spent on the voyage; the remainder has not been paid and will not be paid until the receiving contact comes to the ship
npcs.faction: None. Hired for this run specifically.
npcs.religion: Drift Lord ancestor practice — traveling practitioner version, observes alone
npcs.what_happened: Was approached by a Varathi Return cult intermediary four weeks before the voyage. Survey documentation from an Ossrath interior expedition, double rate, minimal questions. She accepted double rate and asked minimal questions. This is standard cargo practice. The two additional crew members brought aboard by the intermediary — Kern Drath and Sela Voss — she assumed were specialists for the survey documentation. They were below decks for most of the outbound voyage and for the four hours in Keth area when she was told they were 'making a collection.' After the collection, they were wrong. Not sick-wrong — orientation-wrong, which she has never encountered before in twenty-five years.
npcs.the_course_corrections: Three weeks at sea compensating for two crew members who kept finding reasons to adjust the helm toward a heading that was not her destination. She made fourteen documented course corrections in the ship's log. The pattern of the corrections, if plotted, describes an arc toward the submarine ridge that appears on the old Varathi-era chart.
npcs.what_she_wants: To be paid the remainder of her fee. To leave. To never have taken this run.
npcs.what_she_will_do: A player who comes aboard with legitimate purpose and demonstrates they understand what is wrong with her crew will receive her cooperation. She has been carrying this alone for six weeks — the voyage plus the three harbor weeks. The relief of telling someone who understands is greater than the instinct to keep the cargo's nature private.
npcs.player_interaction: Boarding requires legitimate harbor purpose — pilot business, official inspection, or a sufficiently convincing unofficial approach. The camp follower spawn hook finds her at The Tide's End inn when she is ashore. She is the most direct access point to the disc, the chalk scratching, and the cult's receiving operation — she knows the intermediary who hired her and the arrangement for payment upon delivery.

npcs.name: Mael Thornwick
npcs.role: Varathi Return cult — Anchorage operation leader
npcs.location: Rented rooms in three different establishments — the cult's operational security compromise
npcs.physical: Has been a Varathi Return scholar for twelve years and a field operative for approximately three weeks. The scholar's habits are visible — carries books, makes notes, reads in public. These are not habits that support operational security in a harbor environment.
npcs.disposition: Competent at scholarship. Specifically incompetent at the operational requirements of a harbor environment. Does not know what he does not know about harbor operational security, which is the worst possible configuration.
npcs.religion: Varathi Return — genuine, twelve years, the specific faith of someone who believes the recovery of a lost civilization's knowledge is the most important work on Arneth
the_mistakes: Rented rooms in three establishments under names that do not match any Compact registry — harbor registry checks are routine and this is detectable
Asked about harbor piloting schedules in person at the pilot station rather than reading the posted board — pilots notice who asks questions that do not require asking
Made night boat approaches to the outer harbor ship twice — harbor pilots document all night vessel movements
An operative under his command attempted to bribe a dock worker for access to outer harbor berthing records — the dock worker refused and is now talking about it
Made three return visits to the same harbor walk observation point — observable to anyone watching the harbor carefully
npcs.what_he_is_trying_to_do: Receive the disc and the chalk scratching from the ship. The plan: the receiving contact (Oswin Halvwater) smoothes the administrative path. The ship comes to an inner harbor berth. The cargo is transferred in the normal course of unloading. This plan has been delayed for three weeks because the ship's captain has not moved the ship to an inner berth — she has been waiting for the paying contact to come to her in the outer harbor, which the cult operation has not done because coming to the outer harbor means being visible in a way the Compact inspection process would catch.
npcs.the_deadlock: The captain wants to be paid before she moves the ship. The cult wants the ship moved before they come to it. Neither side has resolved this in three weeks. The disc and the scratching are still on the ship.
npcs.player_interaction: His operational mistakes are visible — the petty criminal spawn hook description includes one of them. A player who identifies the cult operation through mistake-observation can approach Thornwick through his rented rooms, through the night approaches, or through the dock worker bribery thread. He is not dangerous in a physical sense. He is dangerous in the specific way that an incompetent person with access to something significant is always dangerous.
